key_event: Trump-Iran diplomatic talks described as 'very good'
US warns ships to avoid Iranian waters during Hormuz talks
Iran insists on maintaining uranium enrichment
US-Israel joint military exercises escalate tensions
Final diplomatic ultimatum issued to Iran
Pre-strike day — market pricing in risk premium
DAY 1: US-Israel launch joint airstrikes on Iran nuclear sites. Ayatollah Khamenei killed. Iran closes Strait of Hormuz. Brent +9%, WTI +8.4%. IRGC: 'Strait is closed'
DAY 2: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Israel. 3 Gulf tankers struck. Brent hits highest since 2025
DAY 3: Qatar suspends LNG production after drone attacks on Ras Laffan and Mesaieed. IEA emergency meeting called
DAY 4: US sinks 16 Iranian minelaying vessels near Hormuz. Dubai cargo aircraft diverted. IEA announces reserve release
DAY 5: Brent +4.76% on tanker attacks. IEA 400M barrel release announced — largest in history. US commits 172M SPR barrels
DAY 6: AAA — national gas average jumps 27 cents in one week. Trump demands allies send warships to reopen Hormuz
DAY 8: Brent crosses $96. Dubai crude highest in history. India scrambles to reroute tankers around Cape of Good Hope
DAY 11: Brent crosses $100 for first time since 2022. Gas hits highest since mid-2024 — up 21% in one month (AAA)
DAY 12: Trump pressures NATO allies to escort tankers. AAA: gas at $3.54 — highest since mid-2024
DAY 13: Saudi Arabia intercepts Iranian drones targeting Aramco. Dubai crude $152+ — 40-year record high
DAY 14: Brent $103. US allows sanctioned Iranian oil tankers through Strait as partial de-escalation measure
DAY 15: Prices ease as Netanyahu signals ceasefire possible. Citi raises forecast to $120 near-term bull case
DAY 16: Trump strikes Iranian Kharg Island military assets. Gas up 80 cents from pre-war level — largest monthly rise since Katrina
DAY 17: Diesel nears $5/gal — highest since 2022. India confirms 2 ships through Hormuz — trickle resuming
DAY 18: AAA national average $3.79 — highest since Sep 2023. US gas prices up 81 cents from pre-war. Diesel $4.94

DAY 19: Israel strikes South Pars — Iran's largest gas field. Iran retaliates: strikes Saudi Aramco Samref refinery and UAE Al Hosn gas field. QatarEnergy 17% LNG capacity offline. Brent $108.65. Gas $3.84 — highest since Oct 2023 (AAA)

DAY 20: Brent briefly hits $119 intraday high on Qatar attack. Closes $108.65. WTI $96.14. Diesel $5.07 — highest since 2022. Netanyahu says Israel helping reopen Hormuz. Dubai drone incident near DXB Airport. 4 injured

DAY 21: Brent open $110.96. WTI $96.20. US weighs releasing sanctioned Iranian tanker crude. Bessent: 'US may free up Iranian oil on water'. Pentagon requests extra $200B for war. Hegseth warns largest strike package yet incoming. Citi: $120 base case 1-3 months
